Highlights from the 2026 British Grand Prix
Before Formula 1 reaches its summer break, two key races in Belgium and Hungary are set to decide which drivers and teams will go into August with momentum.
### Tightened title battle resumes!
After exceeding all expectations over the opening nine rounds, Kimi Antonelli will arrive at this weekend's Belgian Grand Prix with a 25-point lead at the top of the standings over his Mercedes team-mate George Russell.

subtasks.result:
Highlights from the 2026 British Grand Prix.
With a potentially thrilling title battle shaping up as the 2026 Formula 1 season nears its halfway point, Sky Sports F1 assesses the prospects of the main contenders.
The racing resumes this weekend with the Belgian Grand Prix, before the final back-to-back before the sport's summer break is completed by the Hungarian Grand Prix.
With nine rounds complete, Kimi Antonelli holds a 25-point lead at the top of the standings over his Mercedes team-mate George Russell.

subtasks.task: Research and list important events from the latest F1 race
subtasks.result:
Jul 17, 2026, 12:45 PM ET
George Russell finished an alarming eighth in practice for the Belgian Grand Prix as his world championship rival Kimi Antonelli set the pace.
Russell took advantage of Antonelli's failure to finish at the last round in Silverstone to trim the Italian teenager's title advantage to 25 points having been as many as 68 back after last month's Monaco Grand Prix.
However, Russell ended the second session in Spa-Francorchamps trailing Antonelli by a distant 1.2 seconds.
"The rear tyres felt too cold, and I was sliding around a lot, but not 1.2 seconds cold," reported a concerned Russell over the radio.
5hNate Saunders
Everything we know (and don't) about Max Verstappen's future: Stay loyal to Red Bull or time for new start
11hNate Saunders

11hESPN

1dLaurence Edmondson
Verstappen signs McLaren jr to his management
1dLaurence Edmondson
Verstappen: 'Nothing to say' on Red Bull future
1dNate Saunders
Norris gets 10-place Belgian GP grid penalty
1dLaurence Edmondson
Vowles confident in long-term plan for Sainz
4dPA
# Kimi Antonelli dominates George Russell by 1.2 seconds in pivotal Belgian GP practice
- Alonso priorities World Cup final over Spa  
- Piastri denies Verstappen-McLaren swap
Mercedes have secured pole position at every race so far but started the weekend on the back foot, with Antonelli and Russell a distant sixth and eighth in the opening running.
It was Max Verstappen who set the fastest lap in the first session only to finish third by the end of the day with Antonelli taking control and Lando Norris slotting into second place.
Norris is armed with a new battery in Belgium which will cost him a demotion of 10 places on the grid for Sunday's 44-lap race having exceeded his permitted number of engine parts.

Highlights from the 2026 British Grand Prix
Before Formula 1 reaches its summer break, two key races in Belgium and Hungary are set to decide which drivers and teams will go into August with momentum.
### Tightened title battle resumes!
After exceeding all expectations over the opening nine rounds, Kimi Antonelli will arrive at this weekend's Belgian Grand Prix with a 25-point lead at the top of the standings over his Mercedes team-mate George Russell.

subtasks.result:
To compile the information about the winner and important events into a summary, the following is noted:

- The latest completed F1 race is the 2026 British Grand Prix.
- The winner of the latest completed F1 race is not explicitly stated in the prior results.
- Important events from the latest F1 race and recent developments include:
  - Kimi Antonelli holds a 25-point lead at the top of the standings over his Mercedes team-mate George Russell.
  - George Russell finished eighth in practice for the Belgian Grand Prix, 1.2 seconds behind Kimi Antonelli.
  - Other news includes Norris getting a 10-place Belgian GP grid penalty, Verstappen's future being uncertain, and Piastri denying a Verstappen-McLaren swap.

The winner of the 2026 British Grand Prix is missing from the prior results, which is necessary to complete this step accurately.
